requirements for plan eligibility.description: Questions about baseline criteria that must be met to qualify for a plan, such as age, program participation, health status, income, or coverage prerequisites.

general eligibility verification.description: Requests to confirm whether an individual currently meets eligibility criteria for a plan or benefit.

eligibility reapplication.description: Questions about reapplying for eligibility after a prior denial, termination, or loss of qualifying status.

plan specific eligibility.description: Eligibility questions tied to the requirements of a specific named plan rather than general program rules.

medicare eligibility status.description: Questions about whether an individual is eligible for Medicare or currently has Medicare coverage.

medicare part enrollment.description: Questions about eligibility for, or enrollment in, Medicare Parts A, B, C, or D.

medicaid eligibility status.description: Questions about whether Medicaid coverage is active, terminated, pending, or never established.

medicaid level determination.description: Questions about the type or level of Medicaid coverage (e.g., full Medicaid, partial Medicaid, QMB, SLMB).

dual plan eligibility.description: Questions about eligibility for plans requiring both Medicare and Medicaid.

dual eligibility maintenance.description: Questions about maintaining eligibility for dual plans when Medicaid or Medicare status changes.

chronic special needs eligibility.description: Questions about eligibility for Chronic Special Needs Plans (C‑SNP) based on qualifying medical conditions.

low income subsidy eligibility.description: Questions about eligibility for the Medicare Part D Low Income Subsidy (LIS), including whether an individual qualifies based on income, assets, or program participation.

extra help status.description: Questions about eligibility for, approval of, or changes to the Medicare Part D Extra Help program.

income asset eligibility.description: Eligibility questions based on income, assets, spend‑down rules, or financial thresholds.

location based eligibility.description: Questions about eligibility that depend on geographic factors such as state, county, ZIP code, service area, or plan availability in a specific location.

special election eligibility.description: Questions about eligibility triggered by qualifying events such as coverage loss or status changes.

loss of coverage eligibility.description: Eligibility tied to losing employer, private, or government coverage.

medicaid change eligibility.description: Eligibility impacts resulting from a change in Medicaid status, level, or approval outcome.

residency change eligibility.description: Eligibility questions related to moving to a different state, county, or service area and how that affects state‑based programs.

disaster event eligibility.description: Questions about special eligibility provisions following natural disasters or declared emergencies.

medical condition based eligibility.description: Eligibility questions where health conditions affect plan access, benefits, or qualification.

chronic condition verification.description: Questions about documentation or confirmation required to prove a qualifying chronic condition.

military coverage eligibility.description: Questions about eligibility involving VA benefits, Tricare, or military service.

employer coverage eligibility.description: Eligibility questions related to current or prior employer‑sponsored coverage.

employer coverage transition.description: Questions about eligibility changes when moving from employer insurance to Medicare.

other insurance eligibility.description: Questions about eligibility when other insurance is present, such as VA, Tricare, Marketplace, or retiree plans.

eligibility for extra benefits.description: Questions about qualifying for additional benefits such as food, utilities, transportation, or allowance cards.

supplemental benefit eligibility.description: Questions about eligibility for Medicare supplements, Medigap plans, or secondary coverage options.
